description: Data validation set for the Autonomous Liaison Agent prototype — Track 1: Chaos Sector

dil_compliance.complexity: CDC NIOSH 2016-106 (192 pages), Dallas UHI Study (83 pages), DFR EMS Annual Report (27 pages), and OSHA OTM Ch4 (dense technical)
dil_compliance.signal_to_noise: 911 dispatch data contains 10 heat-related MO entries among 1035 total records; social media mixes genuine heat/outage reports with sarcasm and irrelevant posts
dil_compliance.conflict_scenario: NWS uses Heat Index thresholds (Danger at 103F) while OSHA/NIOSH uses WBGT thresholds — leads to conflicting risk assessments for the same conditions

datasets.description: Real Dallas PD dispatch records from Aug 5-27, 2023 heat wave. Sampled evenly across 23 days. Contains incident number, signal code, nature of call, MO narrative, premise, address, beat, division, zip code, and geocoded lat/lon. Agent 1 must identify heat-related signals buried in noise (assaults, thefts, traffic).

Hourly weather data from 8 virtual stations across Dallas zip codes (75201, 75204, 75211, 75215, 75217, 75220, 75228, 75243). Aug 4-27, 2023. Includes temperature, apparent temperature, humidity, dewpoint, wind speed/direction, direct and diffuse solar radiation. Real data with real spatial variation across neighborhoods.

Simulated social media posts during the Dallas Aug 2023 heat wave. Mix of: ~40% genuine heat/outage signals, ~30% ambiguous/sarcasm, ~30% pure noise (ads, sports, food). Data friction applied: 41% of posts missing zip field (force geocoding from text), 26 malformed timestamps (AM/PM, missing T separator), deliberate misspellings of critical keywords (transfomer, outtage, amblance, colapsed, etc.).

Criteria for a Recommended Standard: Occupational Exposure to Heat and Hot Environments. Dense technical document covering WBGT thresholds, physiological heat stress responses, recommended exposure limits, and heat illness prevention. Primary RAG corpus for Agent 2.

OSHA Technical Manual chapter on heat stress. Covers WBGT equations, heat illness types and symptoms, engineering/administrative controls, acclimatization. Includes real-world fatality case study. RAG corpus for Agent 2.

NWS heat index danger classifications and heat advisory criteria. Uses Heat Index (2-variable) rather than WBGT (4-variable). CONFLICT DOCUMENT: NWS thresholds can contradict OSHA/NIOSH WBGT-based thresholds for the same conditions. Agent 2 must reconcile.

FEMA National Incident Management System doctrine. Covers resource typing, incident command system, operational coordination, and mutual aid. Used by Agent 3 to ground resource allocation decisions in official ICS doctrine.
All 60 Dallas Fire-Rescue stations with coordinates, addresses, and zip codes. Used as home locations for asset inventory. Real data from Dallas Open Data GIS layer.

Heat-relevant 311 service requests from Aug 2023. Filtered to: Homeless Encampment, Dead Animal Pick Up, Animal Lack of Care, and Water/Wastewater requests. Complements 911 data — 311 captures slower-burn community heat stress that precedes emergencies. Real data with lat/lon coordinates.

datasets.source: Generated from DFR EMS Annual Report 2023 fleet data + real station locations
datasets.description: Typed asset inventory for Agent 3 dispatch optimization. 47 front-line ALS ambulances, 8 peak-demand single-function rescues, and 1 MODSS outreach team. Each asset has NIMS-style typing (category, kind, type), home station coordinates, coverage radius, capacity, and shift constraints. Asset counts match real DFR fleet; station assignments are synthetic but geographically realistic.

Dallas Fire-Rescue EMS 2023 Annual Report. Documents the exact fleet: 47 front-line ambulances, 8 single-function peak-demand rescues, 883 paramedics, MODSS outreach team, special events division. Agent 3 uses this via RAG to understand fleet constraints and staffing models.

datasets.source: Texas Trees Foundation / Georgia Tech Urban Climate Lab
datasets.description: Dallas Urban Heat Island Management Study (2017). Comprehensive assessment with 4,000+ data points on neighborhood-level heat vulnerability, tree canopy coverage, impervious surface ratios, and public health impacts. Dallas is heating faster than almost any other major US city. Agent 2 uses this via RAG to justify threat scoring for specific Dallas neighborhoods.
